title: TEN Framework: Voice AI That Can Actually Be Interrupted
summary: TEN Framework is an open-source framework for building real-time voice AI agents that handle streaming audio, interruptions, VAD, turn detection, STT, LLM, TTS, avatars, telephony, and multimodal AI workflows. It uses graph-based extensions and real-time audio pipelines to enable natural conversation with interruption support, addressing limitations of sequential STT-LLM-TTS pipelines.
writeup: This video covers TEN Framework, an open-source system for building real-time voice AI agents. It explains how the framework handles streaming audio, voice activity detection (VAD), turn detection, and interruptions, along with STT, LLM, and TTS components, plus avatars, telephony, and multimodal workflows. The core point is that TEN uses graph-based extensions and real-time audio pipelines to support natural conversation with interruption handling, addressing the limitations of sequential STT-LLM-TTS pipelines.
